readingLoading: Preparing your chart… If this message remains, return to the first screen and try again.
readingError: We couldn't display this chart. Please return to the first screen and try again. Your birth details were not sent anywhere.

analytics.consentBody: With your permission, Google Analytics measures privacy-safe page views and interactions. Birth details and chart parameters in result URLs are excluded.

privacyAnalytics.title: Google Analytics
privacyAnalytics.body: Only after you consent does this site load Google Analytics 4 (measurement ID G-RMFH4E7NGS). Google may set analytics cookies for up to 90 days.
privacyAnalytics.data: We send the page path without its query string, the site language, and events such as chart creation, sharing, glossary use, and shop or booking clicks. Birth date, birth time, gender, city, longitude, time zone, and chart inputs are never sent.
privacyAnalytics.control: You can grant or withdraw consent at any time through Analytics preferences in the footer. Refusing analytics does not affect the chart. External booking and shop links have their own privacy policies.

fortune.title: What would you like to explore?
fortune.subtitle: Select a perspective. One birth chart powers every card, and you can switch views after calculation.
fortune.resultSubtitle: Switch perspectives without entering your birth details again. Every summary links to the chart data that supports it.
fortune.basic.title: Core Saju
fortune.basic.body: Start with your Day Master, Four Pillars, Yin-Yang, and Five Elements.
fortune.basic.summary: Your Day Master is {dayMaster}. {element} is the most visible element ({count} of {total}), with {yang} Yang and {yin} Yin positions.
fortune.wealth.title: Wealth flow
fortune.wealth.body: Review Direct and Indirect Wealth, the Wealth element, and its timing.
fortune.wealth.summary: Across visible and hidden stems, {wealthCount} positions belong to the Wealth categories. Your Wealth element is {element}; read its timing through Daeun and annual cycles rather than treating one count as a verdict.
fortune.health.title: Health & balance
fortune.health.body: Explore the traditional Five-Element balance as a wellbeing reflection.
fortune.health.summary: The visible chart leans most toward {dominant}; {lowElements} appear least. This is a traditional Five-Element balance view, not a medical diagnosis or health prediction.
fortune.career.title: Career & achievement
fortune.career.body: Read Authority, Output, the month pillar, and current cycles together.
fortune.career.summary: Across visible and hidden stems, the chart contains {authorityCount} Authority and {outputCount} Output relationships. Read both with the month pillar and current cycles.
fortune.relationships.title: Love & relationships
fortune.relationships.body: Explore the day branch, Peach Blossom, and natal interactions.
fortune.relationships.summary: Your day branch is {dayBranch}. The natal chart has {relationCount} interacting pillar pairs and {starCount} Peach Blossom markers. These describe relationship patterns, not compatibility with a specific person.
fortune.timing.title: Daeun & annual timing
fortune.timing.body: See the current ten-year cycle and this year’s annual pillar.
fortune.timing.summary: Daeun moves {direction}. The current ten-year cycle is {daeunPillar}; the {year} annual pillar is {annualPillar}.

meta.home.title: Show Me the Saju — Your Korean Four Pillars Reading, Free
meta.home.description: Discover your saju (사주), the Korean art of the Four Pillars of Destiny. Get your free chart in seconds — no sign-up.

meta.learn.title: Learn Saju — Show Me the Saju

hero.headline: 1,000 years of Korean destiny reading. Yours in 10 seconds.
hero.sub: Enter your birth details to reveal your Four Pillars — free, no sign-up.
hero.trust: Free · No sign-up · Your birth data never leaves your device

form.timeUnknown: I don't know my birth time
form.trueSolarHint: Adjusts for birthplace longitude (Seoul ≈ −32 min); equation of time is not applied.
form.errFuture: Birth date can't be in the future.
form.errLunarRange: Lunar dates are supported from 1900 to 2050.
form.errSolarCity: Choose a birth city before using longitude-adjusted solar time.

reading.hourUnknown: Hour pillar unavailable without a birth time.
reading.disclaimer: Saju readings are a tradition of self-reflection — not medical, financial, or legal advice.
reading.termBoundaryNote: You were born near a seasonal boundary — your exact birth time matters. Double-check it for an accurate chart.
reading.yinYangSummary: {yang} Yang · {yin} Yin among the visible characters.
reading.dominantSummary: {element} appears most often ({count} of {total} visible positions).
reading.missingSummary: Not visible in the basic chart: {elements}.
reading.noMissingElements: All five elements appear in the basic chart.
reading.elementContext: Counts are a useful first view, not a complete judgment. Season, combinations, hidden stems, and strength can change how an element functions.
reading.tenGodsContext: The Ten Gods are relationship categories, not literal deities. They describe how each visible stem relates to your Day Master.
reading.hiddenStemsContext: Hidden heavenly stems are shown in the Korean order of residual, middle, and main qi. Their Ten Gods are calculated from the Day Master.
reading.relationsContext: These are relationships formed inside the natal chart. They are structural markers, not stand-alone predictions of good or bad fortune.
reading.relationNone: No direct branch relation in the selected set is formed inside the natal chart.
reading.shinsalContext: Shinsal are supplementary traditional markers. They should be read after the overall chart, never as a verdict by themselves.
reading.shinsalNone: None of the major shinsal shown here is present.
reading.daeunContext: Direction follows the year-stem polarity and gender, moving from the month pillar. Start age converts the interval to the adjacent solar-month boundary at three days per year.
reading.seunContext: Each annual cycle runs from Ipchun to the next Ipchun. Select a card to inspect its hidden stems, natal interactions, and shinsal.
reading.daeunApproximate: Because the birth time is unknown, start age is estimated from noon and may differ by about one or two months.
reading.genderRequired: This older shared link has no gender, so Daeun cannot be calculated. Return to the first screen and select gender.
reading.originalChartContext: Compare the four pillars and turn detail layers on or off. The Day pillar is highlighted as the self.
reading.stemRelationNone: No direct heavenly-stem combination or clash is formed.
reading.role.year.title: Year pillar
reading.role.year.body: Often read for ancestry, early environment, and the public or social layer of life.
reading.role.month.title: Month pillar
reading.role.month.body: Connected with season, family environment, work structure, and the chart's overall climate.
reading.role.day.title: Day pillar
reading.role.day.body: Its stem is the Day Master; the pillar is commonly read for self and close partnership.
reading.role.hour.title: Hour pillar
reading.role.hour.body: Often associated with later life, inner aims, projects, and children. It needs an accurate birth time.
reading.readingGuide.title: Read patterns, not isolated scores
reading.readingGuide.body: A responsible reading considers the whole chart. A frequent element is not automatically good, and an absent element is not automatically bad. Use this overview as a map for reflection, then study interactions and seasonal context.
reading.dm.0: Yang Wood (甲) is likened to a tall tree: direct, growth-oriented, and steadied by a clear purpose. Its expression depends on whether the full chart provides roots, water, warmth, and room to develop.
reading.dm.1: Yin Wood (乙) is likened to vines and flowers: adaptive, tactful, and skilled at finding a path around obstacles. Context shows whether that flexibility becomes resilience, diplomacy, or hesitation.
reading.dm.2: Yang Fire (丙) resembles the sun: open, energizing, and inclined to illuminate the wider scene. Balance matters, because strong radiance can inspire while excess heat can overwhelm.
reading.dm.3: Yin Fire (丁) resembles a lamp or candle: focused, perceptive, and able to bring warmth to a specific place. Its steadiness depends on fuel, shelter, and the chart's surrounding climate.
reading.dm.4: Yang Earth (戊) resembles a mountain: stable, protective, and oriented toward endurance. The whole chart shows whether this solidity offers dependable ground or becomes difficult to move.
reading.dm.5: Yin Earth (己) resembles cultivated soil: practical, nurturing, and attentive to gradual improvement. Its qualities emerge through what the chart asks it to receive, organize, and grow.
reading.dm.6: Yang Metal (庚) resembles raw metal or a blade: decisive, resilient, and willing to refine through pressure. Its best expression pairs strength with timing, purpose, and enough flexibility.
reading.dm.7: Yin Metal (辛) resembles jewelry or a fine instrument: precise, discerning, and sensitive to quality. The chart shows whether refinement becomes elegance and clarity or excessive self-critique.
reading.dm.8: Yang Water (壬) resembles an ocean or great river: broad-minded, mobile, and comfortable with large flows of information. Direction and boundaries help its scale become momentum rather than drift.
reading.dm.9: Yin Water (癸) resembles rain, mist, or a spring: subtle, observant, and able to reach hidden places. Its expression is shaped by the channels, warmth, and support found elsewhere in the chart.

saju.name: Saju (Four Pillars)
saju.def: Korea's traditional destiny-reading framework. Birth year, month, day, and hour each form a pillar of two characters—eight in total.

palja.name: Palja (Eight Characters)
palja.def: The eight characters that make up the four pillars. In everyday Korean, palja can also mean one's lot in life.

ilgan.name: Day Master
ilgan.def: The heavenly stem of the day pillar, used as the chart's reference point for the self.

ohaeng.name: Five Elements
ohaeng.def: Wood, fire, earth, metal, and water: five interacting phases used to describe cycles and relationships in the chart.

cheongan.name: Heavenly Stems
cheongan.def: Ten signs that form the upper character of each pillar. Each has an element and a yin or yang polarity.

jiji.name: Earthly Branches
jiji.def: Twelve signs that form the lower character of each pillar and correspond to the familiar twelve zodiac animals.

sipseong.name: Ten Gods
sipseong.def: Ten relationship categories between the Day Master and other stems, used to organize themes such as expression, resources, wealth, and structure.

gunghap.name: Gunghap (Compatibility)
gunghap.def: A comparison of two charts. In Korea it has traditionally been consulted around marriage and partnership.

manseryeok.name: Manseryeok (Perpetual Calendar)
manseryeok.def: A calendar system used to derive the pillars from a birth moment. This site calculates the core chart in your browser.

jinTaeyangsi.name: Longitude-adjusted Solar Time
jinTaeyangsi.def: Clock time adjusted for the birthplace's longitude relative to its time-zone meridian. This site does not apply the equation of time.

ipchun.name: Ipchun (Start of Spring)
ipchun.def: The solar term near February 4 when the Saju year changes. It does not change on January 1 or Lunar New Year.

daeun.name: Daeun (Ten-Year Cycles)
daeun.def: Ten-year cycles advancing from the month pillar. Direction follows year-stem polarity and gender, while the adjacent solar boundary determines the starting age.

about.story: Show Me the Saju makes the structure of a traditional Korean chart easier to explore across languages. The free chart is calculated on your device; a live reading adds the human context, judgment, and conversation that software cannot replace.

learn.title: How to read a Saju chart
learn.intro: Saju (사주), also called the Four Pillars, arranges a birth moment into four pairs of Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches. This guide explains the chart's basic grammar without treating any single symbol as a fixed verdict.
learn.pillars.title: Four pillars, eight characters
learn.pillars.body: The year, month, day, and hour pillars each contain one stem and one branch. Together they form the palja, or eight characters. The hour pillar is omitted when birth time is unknown.
learn.dayMaster.title: The Day Master is the reference point
learn.dayMaster.body: The stem at the top of the day pillar represents the Day Master. Element and Ten-God relationships are described relative to it, but it never tells the whole story alone.
learn.elements.title: Five Elements describe change
learn.elements.body: Wood, fire, earth, metal, and water are interacting phases, not physical ingredients. Generation and control cycles help readers describe support, output, pressure, and transformation.
learn.tenGods.title: Ten Gods organize relationships
learn.tenGods.body: The Ten Gods compare each visible stem with the Day Master by element and polarity. Their names are technical categories—not moral rankings and not literal gods.
learn.solarTerms.title: The chart follows solar terms
learn.solarTerms.body: Year and month pillars are separated by seasonal solar-term boundaries. The Saju year begins at Ipchun near February 4, so it can differ from both January 1 and Lunar New Year.
learn.tradition.title: A living Korean practice
learn.tradition.body: Saju remains part of Korean cultural life, including New Year consultations and gunghap compatibility readings. Methods vary by school and practitioner.
learn.responsible.title: Use the chart for reflection, not certainty
learn.responsible.body: A chart is a traditional interpretive framework, not a scientific diagnosis or guaranteed prediction. Do not use it in place of medical, legal, financial, or mental-health advice.

privacy.leadStrong: Your birth data never leaves your device.
privacy.body1: The saju chart is computed entirely in your browser. We do not collect, transmit, or store birth dates, times, or chart results.
privacy.body2: Anonymous, cookieless visit statistics may be collected through a privacy-focused analytics service. External booking and shop links are governed by their own privacy policies.

explainer.title: What is Saju?
explainer.step1.title: Four Pillars
explainer.step1.body: Your birth year, month, day, and hour each form a pillar — two characters drawn from a 60-unit cycle used in Korea for over a millennium.
explainer.step2.title: Five Elements
explainer.step2.body: Each character carries an element — wood, fire, earth, metal, or water. Their balance shapes your temperament and tendencies.
explainer.step3.title: Your Reading
explainer.step3.body: Interpreting how the pillars interact reveals your strengths, relationships, and timing. Think of it as Korea's answer to the natal chart.

waitlist.title: Want to go beyond the basic chart?
waitlist.body: A live reading can consider combinations, seasonal strength, timing, and the questions that matter to you.

compat.headline: Gunghap (궁합) — Korean compatibility reading
compat.body: For centuries, Korean families checked gunghap before a marriage. Our compatibility engine is in the works.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: This path isn't in your destiny. Try the homepage.
